skill.name: shap
skill.description: Guidance for explaining machine learning models with SHAP. It helps choose explainers, compute SHAP values, select plots, interpret local and global feature importance, and plan debugging, fairness, and production explainability workflows.
skill.summary: Explain machine learning predictions with SHAP workflows, plots, and interpretation guidance.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found they are Markdown syntax, Python SHAP examples, API names, and SHAP terminology. No prompt injection attempt, malicious intent, data exfiltration, or executable marketplace script was found. The only retained risk factor is benign external documentation links, so the skill is safe to publish with low risk.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Static Analysis False Positive
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution appears in Markdown inline code and fenced Python examples for SHAP APIs. No executable skill script, shell interpolation, or user-controlled command execution was found in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are documentation code fences or inline API names. They describe user-run Python SHAP examples and do not define executable marketplace code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Analysis False Positive
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography alerts match SHAP, Shapley, or mathematical notation in model explanation documentation. I found no MD5, SHA-1, DES, insecure random generation, or credential-handling code in the reviewed context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines discuss SHAP and Shapley values, not cryptographic algorithms. The static pattern appears to have matched the SHAP name rather than security-sensitive code.

low_findings.title: Benign Documentation URLs
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE with minor marketplace disclosure value. The hardcoded URLs point to SHAP official documentation and the SHAP GitHub repository. They are reference links, not automated network requests or data exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible documentation links in a resources section. No code path sends user data to these destinations.

content.user_title: Explain Machine Learning Models with SHAP
content.value_statement: Machine learning teams need clear evidence for why models make predictions. This skill provides SHAP workflows, plot selection, and interpretation guidance for local and global model explanations.
actual_capabilities: Selects SHAP explainers for tree, deep learning, linear, and black-box models.
Guides SHAP value computation with background data and model output choices.
Explains waterfall, beeswarm, bar, scatter, force, heatmap, violin, and decision plots.
Provides workflows for model debugging, fairness review, model comparison, and production deployment.
Connects practical interpretation with Shapley value theory and SHAP limitations.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, but it does not run SHAP or train models by itself.
Users must provide their model, data, and installed Python dependencies.
SHAP explains model behavior, not causal truth or policy correctness.
Large datasets and deep models may require sampling, batching, or approximation.

use_cases.title: Explain production model decisions
use_cases.description: Create local and global SHAP explanations for a deployed model and choose plots for stakeholders.

use_cases.title: Debug model behavior
use_cases.description: Use SHAP values to find leakage, unstable features, unexpected interactions, and prediction errors.

use_cases.title: Review fairness signals
use_cases.description: Compare SHAP patterns across cohorts to identify features that may drive uneven model outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Start a SHAP explanation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user with a trained model
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a trained [model type] model and a test dataset. Help me choose the right SHAP explainer and first plots.

prompt_templates.title: Choose plots for stakeholders
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user preparing an explanation report
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend SHAP plots for explaining global feature importance and one individual prediction to a nontechnical audience.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate a model issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me use SHAP to diagnose why my model performs poorly on this subgroup and identify likely data leakage.

prompt_templates.title: Design monitoring workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a production SHAP workflow for batch explanations, latency control, artifact logging, and explanation drift monitoring.

output_examples.input: Explain why a loan model denied one applicant.
output_examples.output: A waterfall explanation plan showing the baseline score, top positive and negative feature contributions, and notes about output units.

output_examples.input: Summarize feature importance for a churn model.
output_examples.output: A beeswarm and bar plot recommendation with guidance on reading feature value colors, ranking, and outlier effects.

output_examples.input: Check whether a model uses sensitive proxies.
output_examples.output: A cohort comparison workflow that reviews SHAP distributions, interaction patterns, and domain validation questions.

best_practices: Choose the most specific explainer supported by the model before using model-agnostic methods.
Use representative background data and document the output units being explained.
Combine global summaries with local examples and validate interpretations with domain experts.
anti_patterns: Treating SHAP values as causal proof without additional causal analysis.
Explaining every row of a large dataset when sampling would answer the question.
Using one plot type for all audiences and model debugging tasks.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps users explain machine learning predictions with SHAP concepts, explainers, plots, and workflows.

faq.question: Can it run SHAP calculations automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides instructions and examples for the user or agent to apply in an existing Python environment.

faq.question: Which models does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers tree models, deep learning models, linear models, and black-box models through the relevant SHAP explainers.

faq.question: Does SHAP prove causality?
faq.answer: No. SHAP explains how a trained model uses features. Causal claims need separate causal evidence.

faq.question: What plots are included?
faq.answer: The skill covers waterfall, beeswarm, bar, scatter, force, heatmap, violin, and decision plots.

faq.question: Is it suitable for fairness reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides workflows for comparing SHAP patterns across cohorts, but human review remains necessary.
